macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Struggles Below 6800 Amid Energy Surge and Sector Weakness
items.summary: The S&P 500 fumbled support at 6800 in a choppy session on March 11, with energy leading gains on surging crude oil while financials broke down and nine of eleven sectors closed lower. Market breadth weakened, Treasury yields rose on fewer expected Fed cuts due to inflation from commodities and geopolitics.[1]
items.source: Investor's Business Daily / Market Misbehavior with Dave Keller

items.title: Crude Oil Volatility Drives Market Swings as Energy Sector Outperforms
items.summary: Crude oil surged with high volatility, boosting energy stocks and the USO ETF up over 35% in a week before pulling back, amid broader market swings of nearly 4%. Utilities held steady as a safe haven, while energy remained strong over the past month despite timing risks.[1][2]
items.source: Investor's Business Daily / Various YouTube Analyses

items.title: Financials and Consumer Staples Deteriorate; Breadth Indicators Weaken
items.summary: Financial sector (XLF, MS, WFC) showed breakdown with failed support, consumer staples rolled over post-failed breakouts (e.g., Conagra), and S&P 500 breadth deteriorated via AD line and bullish percent indexes. Volatility stayed elevated above VIX 24.[1]

items.title: Treasury Yields Rise on Reduced Fed Cut Expectations and Inflation Fears
items.summary: 10-year Treasury yields increased as markets priced in fewer Fed rate cuts amid renewed inflation concerns from rising commodities, soft commodities breakout, and geopolitical tensions. No direct Fed announcements reported.[1]

items.title: Dow Jones Most Punished in Weak Wall Street Close; European Markets Mixed
items.summary: Wall Street closed slightly weak on March 11 with Dow Jones hit hardest, S&P 500 holding 6750-6800 support for third day amid volatility. European indices mixed, with Ibex supported by strong Indictech results.[4]

items.title: Geopolitical Risks from Iran and Strait of Hormuz Add to Oil and Market Volatility
items.summary: Concerns over Iran scenario, merchant mariner issues in Strait of Hormuz, and potential long war driving soaring oil prices are overlooked by Wall Street, contributing to choppy trading and energy surges.[1][4]

items.title: Stock Picks Highlight Oversold Opportunities in AI and Other Sectors Amid Swings
items.summary: Stocks like AppLoving (A, down 45% on AI fears), FICO (down 26% YTD), S&P Global (down 16% YTD), and others seen as buys; utilities and energy resilient, with S&P 500 at 6760 potentially rallying to 7300 before bear market.[2][3]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Wells Fargo Files Trademark for WFUSD Stablecoin
items.summary: Wells Fargo has filed a crypto-related trademark application for WFUSD, indicating a major push into cryptocurrency and blockchain services. This move signals traditional finance's deepening involvement in digital assets.

items.title: Mastercard Launches New Crypto Partner Program
items.summary: Mastercard has introduced a new Crypto Partner Program to support blockchain and crypto integrations. This initiative aims to foster broader adoption among businesses.

items.title: Ledger Partners with Babylon for Bitcoin Vaults
items.summary: Ledger signers will secure Babylon's trustless Bitcoin vaults, allowing holders to lock BTC into programmable contracts while maintaining self-custody. The integration enhances security for Bitcoin staking on Ethereum-compatible chains.

items.title: Bitcoin Faces Potential Breakdown to Test Lows Around $66,000
items.summary: Market analysis warns of Bitcoin potentially breaking down to test recent lows near $66,000, coinciding with a stronger US dollar. Silver and other assets are also under pressure amid broader market trends.

items.title: Strategy (MicroStrategy) Buys Record 3,000 BTC, Holdings Reach 720,000
items.summary: Strategy reportedly purchased 3,000 Bitcoin, boosting reserves to 720,000 BTC and reinforcing its Bitcoin dominance. Official confirmation expected via 8-K filing.

items.title: Visa Expands Stablecoin Payments to 100 Countries
items.summary: Visa is deploying stablecoin payments in 100 countries via Bridge, building on prior crypto settlements and payment cards. This expands crypto spending capabilities globally.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: MIT Leads Charge on AI's Future in Math & Physical Sciences
items.summary: MIT workshop brings together leading AI and science researchers to chart how mathematical and physical sciences can advance AI. Key recommendations highlight need for coordinated investment in computing infrastructure, cross-disciplinary research, and rigorous training to meaningfully advance both AI and science.

items.title: L3Harris and Shield AI Achieve Breakthrough in Autonomous Electronic Warfare
items.summary: L3Harris Technologies and Shield AI successfully demonstrated first-of-its-kind integration combining electromagnetic battle management with autonomous mission software, enabling unmanned systems to detect, analyze and respond to electromagnetic threats in real time without human intervention.

items.title: Deep Learning Market Projected to Reach $306 Billion by 2033
items.summary: Global deep learning market is expected to surge from $44.1 billion in 2026 to $306.3 billion by 2033 with a 31.9% CAGR, driven by strong demand for AI accelerators, cloud platforms, and applications across healthcare, automotive, and cybersecurity.

items.title: AI in Precision Medicine Market Shows Strong Growth with Emerging Genomics Applications
items.summary: AI precision medicine market demonstrates robust growth prospects driven by AI-driven genomics, predictive analytics for early disease detection, and machine learning for drug discovery, though US tariffs create supply chain challenges.

items.title: Finance Leaders Turn AI Experimentation Into Operating Reality in 2026
items.summary: Finance industry shifts from AI experimentation to operational implementation, with improved data governance, continuous controls, and unified evidence pipelines enabling more accurate forecasting and trustworthy AI decision-making.

items.title: NVIDIA Showcases Always-On AI Assistants at GTC
items.summary: NVIDIA demonstrates personalized, always-on AI assistants called 'claws' using the OpenClaw project at GTC, allowing attendees to create and deploy custom AI assistants with expert support.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Future Games Show: Spring Showcase 2026 Happening Today
items.summary: The Future Games Show: Spring Showcase 2026 is airing today at 1:00 PM PT / 4:00 PM ET as a 90-minute digital event featuring over 40 games, including world premieres and new gameplay trailers from both AAA publishers and indie studios.

items.title: Google Play Expands Gaming with Free Trials and Cross-Device Pricing
items.summary: Google Play is introducing game trials for paid titles, a dedicated PC Games section, and a 'buy once, play anywhere' pricing model allowing players to purchase games once and play on both mobile and PC.

items.title: PS Plus March 2026 Game Catalogue Revealed
items.summary: PlayStation Plus has announced its March 2026 game catalogue additions, including Persona 5 Royal and Madden NFL 26.

items.title: NetEase Cuts Funding to Nagoshi Studio
items.summary: Chinese live service giant NetEase has stopped funding Nagoshi Studio, jeopardizing the development of the new Yakuza-inspired game Gang of Dragon and the studio's future.

items.title: Pokémon Pokopia Drives Nintendo Stock Surge
items.summary: Pokémon Pokopia has become a surprise hit for Nintendo, boosting stock prices alongside excitement for the upcoming Super Mario Galaxy Movie releasing April 1.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Synopsys Launches Ansys 2026 R1 with Enhanced Digital Twin and Simulation Capabilities
items.summary: Synopsys launched Ansys 2026 R1 on March 11, 2026, featuring expanded digital twin capabilities, connected modeling workflows, GPU-accelerated sensors, and new broadband 3D power integrity simulation for chip-package integration and advanced packaging.

items.title: Synopsys Unveils Multiphysics Fusion Technology and Integrated EDA Solutions at Converge 2026
items.summary: At Synopsys Converge 2026 on March 11, the company announced Multiphysics Fusion technology integrating Ansys engines into its EDA portfolio for chip design challenges, alongside collaborations with AMD, Intel, Microsoft, and NVIDIA for co-design.

items.title: Synopsys Introduces Software-Defined Hardware-Assisted Verification Platforms
items.summary: Synopsys announced advancements in its hardware-assisted verification portfolio on March 11, 2026, with new software-defined platforms offering improved performance and scalability for verifying multi-die and complex chips.

items.title: Synopsys Announces Electronics Digital Twins Platform at Embedded World
items.summary: Synopsys launched the Electronics Digital Twins (eDT) Platform to connect silicon designs to software and full-system validation, enabling end-to-end digital twin foundations for physical systems development.

items.title: Nvidia Invests $2 Billion in Nebius for Data Center Expansion
items.summary: Nvidia announced a $2 billion investment in Nebius to support new data center development, aiming to diversify cloud options beyond major hyperscalers and promote an end-to-end Nvidia stack for optimized performance.
